description: UAT test scenarios. Each test applies its `setup` op list to a fresh sandbox session, then the user plays through the `instructions` and reports Pass/Fail/Skip + an optional comment. Results append to data/tests/results.jsonl on the server.

ops.reset: Reset sandbox state (empty board, empty hands, fresh player stats).
ops.set_player: {player_idx, field, value} — set a player field (current_mana, max_mana, hp).
ops.add_to_hand: {player_idx, card_id} — add a card to the player's hand.
ops.place: {player_idx, card_id, row, col, [current_health], [dark_matter]} — deploy a minion directly onto the board. Optional current_health pre-damages the minion (min 1).

tests.title: 14.7-09: TURN X / PLAYER X banner stacks on turn flip
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the centred turn banner when the turn flips from P1 to P2.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand.
2. Click any green-tinted tile in the bottom two rows of the board to deploy it.
3. Wait ~1 second. The opponent has no react cards, so the react window auto-closes and the turn flips to P2.
tests.expected: A banner blooms in the centre of the screen with two STACKED lines — 'TURN 2' on top and 'PLAYER 2' directly beneath it (NOT side-by-side). It scales up, holds ~1s, then fades out within ~2s. While visible the banner does not block clicks on the UI behind it.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Fallen Paladin Start: heal blip fires on turn start
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the start-of-turn trigger animation (source-tile pulse + ⏰ glyph at centre) and the resulting +2 self-heal on the Paladin.

Setup state: P1 has a damaged Fallen Paladin on the board (current 30/42 🤍, visible in your bottom area). P2 is the active player on Turn 1 with a Common Rat in hand and 1 mana — your perspective auto-flips to P2.

1. Click the Common Rat in P2's TOP hand. Then click any pink-tinted tile in the TOP two rows to deploy it. (This consumes P2's action and ends the turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes (P1 has no react cards). Watch the Fallen Paladin's tile and the centre of the screen as the turn flips back to P1.
tests.expected: On the turn flip to P1 (TURN 2 banner): the Fallen Paladin's tile briefly pulses yellow, a ⏰ glyph blooms in the centre of the screen, then the Paladin's HP ticks up from 30 to 32 🤍 (Start: passive_heal +2 to itself). No console errors.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Emberplague Rat End: burn-aura blip fires on turn end
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the end-of-turn trigger animation (source pulse → ⏳ glyph → adjacent enemy pulses + becomes burning) when P1's turn ends.

Setup state: P1 has an Emberplague Rat (your bottom area). P2 has a Common Rat one tile in front of it. P1 has a Common Rat in hand and 1 mana — you control P1.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand. Then click any green-tinted tile to deploy it. (This consumes P1's action and ends the turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes (P2 has no react cards). Watch the centre of the screen and the enemy Common Rat tile as P1's End-of-Turn fires.
tests.expected: Before the turn flips to P2: the Emberplague Rat's tile pulses, a ⏳ glyph blooms at centre, then the enemy Common Rat tile pulses. After the flip the Common Rat shows a 🔥 burn badge. No console errors.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Priority modal opens when 2+ same-player triggers fire
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the priority-pick modal that appears when two of P1's start-of-turn triggers fire on the same turn flip.

Setup state: P1 has TWO damaged Fallen Paladins on the board (each at 30/42 🤍). P2 is the active player on Turn 1 with a Common Rat in hand and 1 mana — your perspective auto-flips to P2.

1. Click the Common Rat in P2's TOP hand. Then click any pink-tinted tile in the TOP two rows to deploy it. (Ends P2's turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes. As the turn flips back to P1, a priority-pick modal opens titled 'Pick the order to resolve these effects' showing TWO Fallen Paladin cards.
3. Click one Paladin card. That trigger fires (blip + heal that Paladin from 30 to 32). The remaining trigger has no other choices to compete with, so it auto-resolves and the modal closes.
tests.expected: Modal opens with two full-art Fallen Paladin cards, styled like the tutor modal (cyan border, dark header bar reading 'Pick the order to resolve these effects' and '2 triggers waiting'). Clicking either card fires that Paladin's blip + heal (30→32 🤍). The remaining trigger then auto-resolves (second Paladin also heals 30→32 🤍) and the modal closes cleanly. Control returns to P1; both Paladins on the board now show 16/32 🤍.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Spell-stage fades with 👍 after react drains
tests.instructions:
GOAL (regression): the spell-stage animation must close cleanly in sandbox (it used to hang open with the placeholder still visible).

Setup state: P1 has Acidic Rain (5 mana) in hand and 5 mana available. P2 has a Red Diodebot on the board.

1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's bottom hand. The card highlights with a glow (confirm-armed mode).
2. Click Acidic Rain a SECOND time to confirm the cast.
3. A spell stage appears in the centre: a giant Acidic Rain card slides in from the left, with a cyan ▶ arrow and a placeholder (⚡) on the right. The opponent has no react cards so the react window auto-drains.
4. Watch the spell stage close.
tests.expected: After the auto-drain (~1s): the Acidic Rain card slides off to the right, the placeholder flips to a green 👍 (thumbs-up), and the whole stage fades out within ~1s. The Red Diodebot on the board becomes burning (🔥 badge). The stage does NOT hang open with a placeholder still visible.

tests.title: React chain — Prohibition vs Prohibition, 3 deep
tests.instructions:
Both players have plenty of mana and three Prohibitions each. You'll drive both sides via the 'Controlling' toggle in the sandbox sidebar.

1. Controlling P1, click Acidic Rain in P1's hand and confirm the cast (2nd click).
2. The spell stage shows [Acidic Rain] ▶ [?]. Toggle Controlling → P2.
3. Click Prohibition in P2's hand. The stage should push Acidic Rain LEFT off-screen and slide Prohibition into the centre with a fresh [?] slot.
4. Toggle Controlling → P1. Click Prohibition in P1's hand. Another slide.
5. Toggle Controlling → P2. Click Prohibition in P2's hand. Another slide.
6. Toggle Controlling → P1. No Prohibition left — click the Skip/Pass button in the react window to close the stack.
tests.expected: The spell stage animates a slide-left / slide-in-right each time a react is added. The '?' resets between reacts. Once the stack resolves (LIFO): the topmost Prohibition negates the second, the second negates the first, the first negates Acidic Rain. No minion ends up burning, all 4 cards (Acidic Rain + 3 Prohibitions) land in the Grave piles (Acidic Rain + P1's 2 Prohibitions in P1 grave, P2's 1 Prohibition in P2 grave). No HP damage popup — the spell was negated.

tests.title: Sacrifice — jumper leaps, shatters mid-arc (default visual)
tests.instructions:
1. P1's Common Rat is pre-placed on row 4 — ready to jump off.
2. Click the Rat to select it.
3. Click the Sacrifice action button.
tests.expected: Beat 1: white-purple flash erupts on the tile. Beat 2: the pixel-art jumper silhouette (purple outline, white glow) leaps forward toward P2's HP. Beat 3: mid-arc the silhouette explodes into 8 hexagonal purple shards that fly outward spinning and fade. HP damage popup fires after. A+C combined — this is the default.

tests.title: Acidic Rain burns all Metal/Robot/Machine minions — and nothing else
tests.instructions:
1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's hand.
2. Click any valid play indicator to cast it (it's 5 mana; P1 has 20).
tests.expected: All three Diodebots, the RGB Lasercannon, and the Sparkbot on P2's side should light up as BURNING (look for the fire glow/border). The Common Rat on P2 must NOT be burning.

tests.title: Live Dark Matter count in card text
tests.instructions:
1. Hover (or click) Dark Matter Barrage in P1's hand.
2. Read the rules line.
tests.expected: The line should read 'Deal 5 + 9 (Dark Matter) damage to all enemies' (approximately) with the number 9 glowing in PURPLE. Each of P1's three Mages has 3 Dark Matter stacks, summing to 9.

tests.title: Magic cast animation — card flies from hand to grave
tests.instructions:
1. Play Dark Matter Stash from P1's hand (5 mana).
2. Watch the card as it leaves the hand.
tests.expected: The Dark Matter Stash card should visibly fly from its hand slot upward and land on the P1 Grave pile (bottom-right pile buttons), shrinking and fading as it goes. It should NOT just disappear instantly.

tests.title: Spell cast center stage — magic card appears in centre with ▶ ? slot
tests.instructions:
1. Play Dark Matter Stash from P1's hand (5 mana).
2. Watch the center of the screen immediately after.
tests.expected: A giant Dark Matter Stash appears in the middle of the screen with a cyan ▶ arrow and a '?' slot to its right. After roughly 1 second the '?' flips to a green 👍 (thumbs-up) and the whole overlay fades out.

tests.title: Feed the Shadow — magic spell with destroy-ally cost actually casts
tests.instructions:
1. P1 has 2 mana, a Common Rat at (3,2), and Feed the Shadow in hand.
2. Click Feed the Shadow (with only one legal target the cast auto-resolves).
tests.expected: The card casts without an 'Illegal action' error. Your Common Rat at (3,2) is destroyed. The enemy Rat takes damage equal to the destroyed Rat's attack (10). Your Gargoyle Sorceress (Dark Mage) on the board gains +1 Dark Matter.

tests.title: Gargoyle Sorceress — player picks BOTH discard cards
tests.instructions:
1. Click Gargoyle Sorceress in P1's hand.
2. Pick any empty back-two-row tile to deploy.
3. The picker modal should appear showing ALL 3 other hand cards.
4. Click the first card you want to exhaust. Modal stays open; the card becomes dimmed with a ✓.
5. Click a second card. As soon as you hit the required count (2), Gargoyle deploys and both cards go to the Exhaust pile.
tests.expected: The picker modal does NOT submit after just one click. It waits for 2 picks (progress shows '1 / 2 picked' then '2 / 2 picked'). Clicking an already-picked card un-picks it. After the second pick the Gargoyle Sorceress appears on the chosen tile and exactly the 2 cards you picked land in P1's Exhaust pile.

tests.title: All pickers share the same visual style (tutor is the reference)
tests.instructions:
1. Play 'To The Ratmobile!' from P1's hand (3 mana; tutors 2 Rats from deck). Observe the tutor modal — note its chrome (cyan border, dark header bar with title, card grid, close button).
2. Click Reset in the test panel.
3. Play Gargoyle Sorceress from P1's hand (5 mana) — it needs to discard 2 cards. Observe the discard picker modal.
tests.expected: The discard picker modal uses the SAME visual chrome as the tutor modal — cyan border, dark header bar with title text, card grid body, consistent spacing. The words 'sacrifice' should NOT appear anywhere; this is a DISCARD (cards go to Exhaust). Title should read 'Pick 2 cards to Discard'.

tests.title: Matter Possessed — Cost: Deal 25🤍 to own face
tests.instructions:
1. Hover Matter Possessed in P1's hand and confirm the cost line reads 'Cost: Deal 25🤍 to own face'.
2. P1 HP is 30 and mana is 1.
3. Play Matter Possessed onto any empty tile in P1's back two rows.
tests.expected: P1's HP drops from 30 to 5 (dealt 25 to face). A 25/25 Dark Mage minion appears on the chosen tile. Mana drops from 1 to 0.
